commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Wheat $5.87/bu (Apr 17). USDA raised season-average price estimates. Iran Hormuz opening may ease shipping pressure. Fertilizer crisis persists ($650+/MT urea). US wheat acreage projected at lowest since records began in 1919.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Corn $4.14/bu (Apr 17). USDA raised price estimates. Corn futures saw 4.75-8.25 cent gains on the day. Hormuz opening could ease fertilizer supply pressure.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Soybeans ~$11.60/bu (Apr 17, 4-week high). Supported by firm biofuel-linked demand as crude oil disruptions boost biodiesel. Soybean oil $0.669/lb. USDA raised price estimates.
commodities.source: Brownfield Ag News / CME March 20 closing

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: Sugar #11 (ICE) at $0.224/lb (April 2). Prices reversing earlier decline as shipping costs and diesel surcharges ripple through global supply chains. Hormuz closure driving up transport costs even for non-Gulf commodities. Sugar now modestly above pre-war levels after lagging other commodities.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,535/tonne (April 11, down 2.33% — five-week rally snapped). Indonesia CPO IDR 16,049/kg.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Arabica $2.94/lb (Apr 10). Down from 2025 peak of $4.41 but still elevated. Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.note: USDA projects 3.6% overall food price increase in 2026 (food-at-home +2.5%, food-away-from-home +3.7%). Sugar/sweets +6.7%. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points. Fertilizer crisis (urea +40%, ammonia +41%) threatening Northern Hemisphere planting season.

fertilizer.detail: Approximately one-third of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait of Hormuz. Urea surged to $650+/MT (+~50% in 3 weeks, some spot markets $800+). Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. USDA projects 3.1% food price rise in 2026. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points. Critical Northern Hemisphere spring planting season underway.

context: Day 49 PM: Oil settled $90.38 Brent — biggest crash since April 8. Hormuz declared open but Maersk needs guarantees. If Hormuz reopens fully, fertilizer and food supply chains could begin normalizing. USDA raised wheat, corn, soy price estimates. Wheat $5.87. Fertilizer crisis persists (urea $650+/MT, nitrogen +30-40%). US wheat acreage at record low since 1919. USDA projects food-at-home +3.6% for 2026. Ceasefire expires April 22.
